response.context: Slice SL-002 defines the architecture baseline for opening any prioritized dashboard health item into a detail view, presenting the canonical item guidance and status context, allowing Done completion for eligible items, and reflecting the persisted result in dashboard placement and displayed health score for the active profile.
decisions: Use the profile-specific health item as the canonical aggregate for this slice. It must contain: item id, profile id, action text, recommendation frequency text, why-it-matters text, unified status, priority horizon, and completion context fields needed to render last-known status and persist Done.

Keep the unified status model as a single shared enum/value set with exactly three states: Due, Planned, and Done. The same status values must be used by dashboard data, detail data, completion logic, and any score recalculation input for this slice.
Define completion eligibility as status in {Due, Planned}. A health item already in Done is read-only in this slice and must not expose a Mark Done action.

Model Mark Done as a state transition on the profile health item aggregate: Due -> Done or Planned -> Done. The transition must persist a completion timestamp or equivalent completed context so the detail view can immediately render Done status after save and after reload.

Treat last-known status context as derived presentation data from the persisted profile health item state, not as separate freeform user-entered content. Minimum supported contexts for this slice are: Due shows due-now style context, Planned shows planned context, and Done shows completed context using persisted data.

Open detail views by stable health item identifier from the dashboard, not by dashboard group position or list index. This prevents coupling between dashboard ordering and detail retrieval and supports reopening the same item after state changes.

Place all detail read and write logic inside the new feature module at src/features/health-item-detail-and-completion/. The dashboard module may navigate to the detail route and refresh its own query state, but it must not own completion rules or duplicate status mapping logic for this slice alone via route handlers in
guardrails: Do not introduce new status labels, sub-states, or reminder-related states in this slice. Only Due, Planned, and Done are valid status terms.

Do not create a separate dashboard-specific or detail-specific item model that redefines business rules. Detail and dashboard must read from the same canonical profile health item state or projections derived from it.

Do not store dashboard group membership as an independent mutable field if it can drift from status. Priority presentation after completion must come from canonical status-aware query logic so an item cannot remain in multiple groups.

Do not let the UI optimistically invent a new health score value. The displayed score after completion must come from the same scoring source already used for the profile dashboard, refreshed after the persisted item transition.
Do not expand this feature into reminder scheduling, plan list management, vaccination behavior, or family profile switching. Those remain outside the slice boundary.
Do not add medical-record style history capture. For this slice, only the minimal completion context required to show Done and persist it across reload is allowed.
verification: Route-level test verifies every dashboard item link resolves to a detail view using the item's stable identifier and renders action, recommendation frequency, why it matters, and status context.
Domain or application tests verify Mark Done is allowed only for items currently in Due or Planned and rejected or hidden for items already in Done.
Integration test verifies marking a Due item Done persists the new Done state, shows completed context on the detail view, and still shows Done after page reload in the same test account.
Integration test verifies marking a Planned item Done persists the new Done state, shows completed context on the detail view, and still shows Done after page reload in the same test account.
Dashboard refresh test verifies that after completion the affected item is not rendered in more than one priority group and is removed from prioritized dashboard groups for the active profile.
Score refresh test verifies that after completion the displayed health score changes consistently through the shared score read path rather than a local UI-only calculation.

Regression test verifies the slice 1 onboarding-to-dashboard flow still lands on a generated dashboard and dashboard items remain openable into the SL-002 detail route without semantic/bootstrap issues.
constraints: Scope is limited to dashboard item detail and completion for the active profile generated from onboarding; no additional list screens or cross-profile architecture changes are permitted.

Implementation artifacts must remain within the slice targets: src/features/health-item-detail-and-completion/, src/routes/health-item-detail-and-completion*, and tests/health-item-detail-and-completion/, with only minimal touchpoints to existing dashboard and shared data access code.
The product remains a preventive navigator, not a medical record; persist only minimum data required for status, completion context, and score refresh.
The detail view must always present the canonical content fields required by acceptance: action, why it matters, recommendation frequency, and status context.
Completion must be persisted in the primary data store used by the test account so the updated Done state survives a full page reload.
Health score behavior in this slice must reuse the existing calculation contract and update path; this slice must not redefine score semantics or explanation copy.
Priority grouping remains dashboard-only presentation. The detail feature may display current status and context, but it must not introduce a second grouping system or alternate horizon taxonomy.
open_questions: No open architectural questions for this slice.
